case_title: SIAM GEO ENTERPRISES COMPANY, LIMITED, DBA KOMODO VS. BENJAMIN APPENZELLAR et al

docket_entries.description: REQUEST TO APPEAR AT HEARING BY TELEPHONE FILED BY DEFENDANT APPENZELLAR, BENJAMIN

REMOVED FROM MASTER COURT CALENDAR SET FOR JUL-21-2014 - ***CASE IS DISMISSED*** RUSSELL ROBINSON SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. CHRISTINE PHAM APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF'S ORAL REQUEST TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE IS DENIED BECAUSE THERE IS NO GOOD CAUSE. PURSUANT TO THE FAXED DECLARATION OF PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL, CLAUDIO KOREN, HE HAS LOST CONTACT WITH HIS CLIENT WHO IS A CORPORATION LOCATED IN THAILAND. THE COURT GRANTED DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO DISMISS PURSUANT TO 583.410 CCP. JUDGE: CYNTHIA M. LEE; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BENJAMIN APPENZELLAR'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION BY ALLEGING THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT ACCORDING TO THE CONTRACT'S LEGAL EFFECT. SEE SCOLINOS V. KOLTS, (1995) 37 CAL.APP. 4TH 635, 640. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT BENJAMIN APPENZELLAR'S MOTION TO: 1) DEEM REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED, 2) FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS OFF CALENDAR AS UNTIMELY UNDER CCP 1005(B). THE MOTION WAS FILED AND SERVED ON JUNE 25, 2014. MOVING PARTY ONLY PROVIDED 4 COURT DAYS NOTICE. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED. (302)
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR FILED BY DEFENDANT APPENZELLAR, BENJAMIN

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BENJAMIN APPENZELLAR'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 9, 2013 TO GIVE THE PARTIES THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B AND 2.6C. THE PARTIES MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND OPPOSITION PAPERS AND MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE COMPLAINT TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 8, 2013 AT 4:00 PM. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-21-2014 Dismissal - No Appearance

Defendant Benjamin Appenzellar'S Motion To: 1) Deem Request For Admissions Admitted, 2) For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-02-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christine Pham, Esq., 408.321.8511, for defendant Benjamin Appenzellar; No other appearances.

RULING: Matter discussed. DEFENDANT BENJAMIN APPENZELLAR's Motion To: 1) Deem Request For Admissions Admitted, 2) For Monetary Sanctions is off calendar as untimely under CCP 1005(b). The motion was filed and served on June 25, 2014. Moving party only provided 4 court days notice. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Notice Of And Motion To: 1) Deem Request For Admissions Admitted, 2) For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Jul-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter, the court vacated its tentative ruling and ruled as follo0ws:
DEFENDANT BENJAMIN APPENZELLAR's Motion To: 1) Deem Request For Admissions Admitted, 2) For Monetary Sanctions - Transferred to be heard at the 9:30 a.m. calendar on the same day. Judge: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/jpt

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-07-2014 Set For Trial On Jul-21-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. THOMAS J. SEBOURN OF THE GREENBAUM LAW GROUP, LLP, TEL. NO. (949) 760-1400 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. NAM LE OF THE JAURIGUE LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. (818) 630-7280 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BENJAMIN APPENZELLAR'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges the breach of contract cause of action by alleging the terms of the contract according to the contract's legal effect. See Scolinos v. Kolts, (1995) 37 Cal.App. 4th 635, 640. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-08-2013 Continued To Dec-09-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT BENJAMIN APPENZELLAR's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to December 9, 2013 to give the parties the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B and 2.6C. The parties must deliver courtesy copies of the moving and opposition papers and moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the Complaint to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than November 8, 2013 at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the reply papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/EHG)
